programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate the fake Botox treatment that left one woman with life changing injuries. Elsewhere, a report on false online reviews that are ruining business across the UK, deadly fake camping stoves that can explode when they are lit, and Michelle tests imitation Airpods headphones(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. In an effort to confront their money-spending ways, Alex and Steph resort to shock tactics, and set up a pop-up-shop in the family home displaying all of their biggest spends, with shocking results. Can Alex and Steph help this family start saving, rather than spending?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: A Buddhist painter battles the elements at Boudhanath Stupa in Nepal to honour Buddha's birthday, and in Iraq, at Lalish Temple, a young Yazedi woman goes in search of comfort after being traumatised by the recent conflict. At the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem - where a miracle is believed to happen at the same place, at the same time every year - an orthodox Christian delivers a holy flame into the hands of a bishop. Documentary showcasing what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth.(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

Anita also meets up with a plucky pal with an interesting musical collection from a legendary entertainer(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Fearsome Cuban crocodiles Fang and Keo have just taken up residence at Longleat Safari Park. Ben Fogle and Kate Humble reveal how the crocs' cousins in the wild are known for their leaping prowess - propelling themselves out of the water to reach heights of two metres to snatch prey from overhanging branches. Plus, tragedy strikes amongst the two rare wombats, the only ones of their kind in Europe(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a clothing factory in South Shields that manufactures 650 waxed jackets a day. Cherry Healey investigates the science behind waterproof clothing, discovering how a layer of material can keep rain in while allowing sweat to escape, as well as discovering 150-year-old methods of making umbrellas. Plus, Ruth Goodman visits a Scottish harbour to discover the origins of weatherproof coats(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Lindsey Chapman and Martin Hughes-Games join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Following the experiences of families, couples and individuals as they swap homes with complete strangers around the UK, beginning with households from Wiltshire and Falmouth(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: An unexpected discovery in the case of a postman leads to a potential windfall, while detective work reveals an enormous family tree. Presented by Michael Buerk(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of sugar-rich foods in Britain, and try to make their own versions of their favourite childhood sweets. The duo also prepare a cheesecake and a sweet-and-sour dish(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: An emergency Caesarean on a cow leads to a worrying case for farm vet Graham, and Ian performs a dangerous operation on a rescue dog with painful ears. At a small animal hospital in Fife, a local celebrity - the town peacock - puts the pressure on vet Adam. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Animals Unexpected
programme.desc: Biologist Lucy Cooke investigates reports of animals being found in unusual places, often turning up thousands of miles from their natural habitats. She goes on a journey around the world to discover why these creatures have relocated, examining the unusual skills animals use to adapt to new surroundings and how humans have altered the environment, with unforeseen consequences(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Newcomer Angus Ashworth and old hand David Harper set off on a search around Stirling and Perth, buying items along the way to sell at an auction in Dundee(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Comedians Ken Cheng, Hal Cruttenden, Justin Moorhouse, Zoe Lyons and Ruth Bratt take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on train journeys through Edwardian Britain, exploring the grand estates of Norfolk and a bivouac on Brownsea Island on the way. He also learns the ropes aboard an Edwardian wherry on the Norfolk Broads(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a concertina that survived combat in the First World War, a vintage jigsaw, and a cocktail table with a surprising secret compartment. Plus, soft-toy restoration duo Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch are called upon to rescue a much-loved toy dog, made by German manufacturer Steiff, which its owner Anita has treasured ever since it was given to her by her grandparents as a young girl(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Colourful milkshake funnel cakes have all the fun of fresh doughnuts with none of the fuss. A giant time-saving cookie is so easy it's fried in the pan, and a glistening nutty baklava looks so impressive but is completely fool-proof. On the road, Nadiya joins the team Tate and Lyle on the River Thames, to find out how they produce one of her favourite fast-tracks to flavour - golden syrup(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The sixth first-round match sees four students from the University of Edinburgh pit their wits against a team from Birkbeck, University of London in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Day Mountbatten Died
programme.desc: On 27 August, 1979, Lord Louis Mountbatten, great uncle to Prince Charles, was blown up at sea by the IRA off the west coast of Ireland. Three others were killed on the boat that day, including two teenage boys, and later that afternoon the IRA killed 18 British soldiers across the border in Northern Ireland. Forty years on, this is the story of that remarkable Bank Holiday Monday told by those directly affected by it(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Actor Richard E Grant looks back at the year when he starred in cult classic Withnail & I, and Rick Astley, Terence Trent D'Arby, Run DMC and the Beastie Boys all hit the charts. TV screens saw the arrival of US drama Thirtysomething, while Damon and Debbie eloped in Brookside(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Japan's Secret Shame
programme.desc: Documentary about Shiori Ito, a Japanese journalist who created controversy when she went public with her allegations that she was raped by a famous TV personality and writer. The film follows her over the course of a year, as she pursues a civil case following the collapse of criminal charges, and explores how discussion of sexual assault is strictly repressed in Japanese culture, leaving many victims too afraid to share their stories(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The year 1973 finds Bob juggling dealing with the success of Cabaret, spearheading production on Liza with a Z and Pippin and grappling with the price of fame. Though he is indulging in the high life, with several girlfriends, pills and poppers, he finds time to attend the opening night of Gwen's Broadway play Children! Children! Gwen tearfully tells him that the play is closing after only one performance, since the producers have had word of bad notices and poor box office. She implies that if Bob had helped with rewrites, the production might have been saved. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Perthshire, where Matt Baker visits Cultybraggan, a former PoW camp that is now a thriving community of artisans, craftspeople and food producers. Charlotte Smith jumps in a kayak and takes to the water on a wildlife safari, while Steve Borwn meets women growing tea in the south Highlands and learns the art of making the perfect cuppa(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Comedy actors Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer travel to the picturesque River Usk in south Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. The duo discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives and values. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar. Once outside in the picturesque environment, their quarry is the wild brown trout(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and the team traces the family of Charlie Graves in time for his funeral. They also research the case of Roy Panther, a man who left his mark shining in the universe(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey through time by celebrating Britain's numerous food festivals. The duo reveal the fascinating stories behind the events and sample some of the dishes on offer(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives and work of rural vets. In the first edition, farm vet Richard helps a sheep who has suddenly gone blind in Teesdale, and a spaniel proves a handful for small animal vet Steve. Plus, Adam deals with the case of a homeless kitten run over by a car. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Mongoose start wars to create a diversion for sneaky mating, while macaques kidnap babies to curry favour with their rivals. These behaviours prompt Liz Bonnin to investigate the animals who will stop at nothing to find a mate and raise a family. Liz reveals the fascinating science that lies behind some extraordinary tactics. In Tanzania, she finds out that sibling rivalry can actually benefit a whole hyena clan, and on the North American plains learns how promiscuity can be the key to having healthy prairie-dog pups. However, the presenter also discovers that such tactics are often the key to survival(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: David Harper and Angus Ashworth continue their Caledonian capers with a pile of old luggage, an adorable canine, a silver topped cane and a shot on the golf course(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sports presenters Mike Bushell, Hugh Woozencroft, Holly Hamilton, Sonali Shah and Jessica Creighton take on the general-knowledge champions(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Letchworth Garden City to Herne Hill
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores an Edwardian utopia in Letchworth, where he meets a fourth generation citizen who introduces him to the city's community spirit. He then heads towards London, and discovers a favoured haunt of King Edward VII, before sampling the monarch's favourite tipple, the King's Ginger(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a rusty toy pedal car that is missing its steering wheel, a Victorian stamp perforating machine, a bronze statue of a dagger-wielding assassin by leading French artist Claire Colinet, and a baize-covered card table dating from the 1940s that has sentimental value for its owner(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a mattress factory in Leeds, where he discovers how steel is stretched and coiled into springs and the advantages of natural fibres like hemp and wool. Cherry Healey talks to a sleep scientist about the benefits to reaction time from taking an afternoon nap, and Ruth Goodman tries out a Middle Ages mattress made out of a straw-stuffed sack(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the brigade to San Sebastian in the Basque region of Spain, where they face their toughest challenge to date competing against the kitchen staff of Michelin-starred chef Paulo Airaudo. Jason has had to bring in two new recruits to replace the chefs who have dropped out, and they struggle to find their place on the team - especially as both are used to being in charge(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Paul Chowdhry, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Milton Jones and Rachel Parris and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan spends a week exploring Colombia with a local radio host and journalist, discovering how much of the country's reputation for violence is justified. He investigates a new trend for narcotourism, where visitors seek out places associated with drug lord Pablo Escobar, and finds out how Colombians feel about foreigners' fascination with their nation's most infamous criminal. He also visits a Jesus statue shop, learns to make coffee the traditional way, and discovers a game involving explosions(n)

programme.title: Broke
programme.desc: Further real-life stories of people who are in work but struggling to make ends meet. Intensive-care nurse Lorraine has not had a pay rise in eight years and with inflation and the rising cost of living, she earns thousands of pounds less after tax than in 2010. Kevin from North Ayrshire works more than 100 hours a week managing four businesses, yet he still delivers takeaways at night to save for his son's long-term future, while Eryl in Liverpool tops up her zero-hour wage by foraging for foliage and making wreaths to sell(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police's Extradition Unit search for two people convicted of slavery and extortion offences in Italy, but DS Pete Rance and his team decide to take a cautious approach as the pair have a history of violence. In Derbyshire, the team are on the road before dawn looking for a man wanted in Slovakia, while detectives in south Wales work with Tanzanian police to locate a murderer on the run in Africa(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life
programme.desc: An ex-commando is on a mission to represent Great Britain as an elite athlete, and a couple struggle with the gruelling process of IVF, having failed twice before(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: A Community Land Trust tries to control house prices and prevent locals from being priced out of the neighbourhoods. Plus, the story of an artisan baker's rise to success(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: A vet battles to save the life of a pregnant deer and its unborn calf, which is in a dangerous breach position. Jean Johansson finds out how fast a chameleon's tongue can move(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they learn the basic skills needed to face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In California, a three-month old sea otter pup has to learn what's safe to eat in a world enmeshed with humans, while in Sri Lanka, a seven-month old macaque is forced by his mother to toughen up. In Kenya, a five-month old elephant bond with new families(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts as team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Rachel Parris and Hugh Dennis for another test of wildlife knowledge(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Two families from Yorkshire and Cumbria swap houses, experiencing life in a different location, and putting their own homes and lifestyles to the test(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The team uncovers one of Britain's worst transport disasters while tracing heirs to an estate, and also try to find relatives of a man involved in greyhound racing in its prime(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Neil Forster is an impatient cook who reaches for the takeaway menu rather than making food from scratch. Lorraine shows him recipes including asparagus and cream cheese puff pastry tarts, a quick way to do a roast chicken and a neat shortcut to prepare a summer fruits pudding(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers indulge in an afternoon snack as they examine the history of high tea in Britain. The pair sample a selection of tasty food and hear some fascinating stories as they embark on a quest to explore what has become a national institution(n)

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: In the second episode, Liz Bonnin meets the animals doing whatever it takes to find food, joining experts to uncover the science behind their ingenious tactics and some of their unusual behaviour. Liz sees macaques using psychology to pull off a theft, tigers deceiving their prey, a wedge-billed hummingbird stealing nectar from under the beaks of its rivals, a spider conning prey with a chemical disguise, and coconut crabs who seem intent on a stealing spree(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and David Harper salvage chimney pots, vintage cases and an old washtub before they head to their next auction in Bedale, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of experts from the worlds of history, science and antiques comprising of Geoffrey Munn, Tessa Dunlop, Curtis Dowling, Simon Singh and Anita Anand sets out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At the Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Aspiring designers take on commercial interior challenges, as they compete to win a life changing contract. The nine remaining contestants are challenged to use their interior design skills to transform hotel bedrooms at the Chocolate Boutique Hotel in Bournemouth. The hotel has a strong chocolate theme throughout, so the competitors, split into teams of three, must incorporate dark, milk or white chocolate in their designs. At the end of the challenge, one of the contestants is sent home. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at criminal investigations. After 46-year-old Stephen Wallace is found dead in his Paisley home from a number of injuries, detectives forensically comb the crime scene for clues which will lead them to his killer(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam feels unappreciated when her daughters prefer to watch RuPaul than her own show. Comedy drama, created by and starring Pamela Adlon. Also stars Olivia Edward, Hannah Alligood and Mikey Madison(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the Portuguese city of Lisbon, where he is inspired by the city's love of seafood to cook his own take on salt cod fritters(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the brigade to San Sebastian in the Basque region of Spain, where they face their toughest challenge to date competing against the kitchen staff of Michelin-starred chef Paulo Airaudo. Jason has had to bring in two new recruits to replace the chefs who have dropped out, and they struggle to find their place on the team - especially as both are used to being in charge(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Snowdonia during the autumn, finding red squirrels on the lower slopes of the Carneddau range and jays collecting acorns in ancient woodland near Aber Falls. He also discovers old sheepfolds and Wales' native black cattle breed on the high slopes - both relics of a time when many more people lived and worked on the uplands and evidence of Snowdonia's changing nature(n)

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The strategies adopted by plants that enable them to grow in every possible environment, even where there is no sunlight, water or nutrients. Featuring the dragon's blood tree, which survives in the desert by converting moisture found in the mist and shading its own roots, the carnivorous Venus flytrap that has evolved into an extraordinary predator able to digest insects, and the cat's claw creeper, which uses other plants as a ladder to reach sunlight. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Rams: Principles of Good Design
programme.desc: A profile of German Dieter Rams, whose work for consumer products manufacturer Braun and furniture company Vitsoe influenced Apple, but who now regrets being a designer(n)

programme.title: Sir Chris Hoy: From Velodrome to Le Mans
programme.desc: Documentary following the Olympic cyclist as he retrains as a racing driver to pursue a lifelong dream of competing in the world's toughest endurance race, the Le Mans 24 Hours. His new sporting career is nearly brought to an abrupt end when the team he has joined is forced to withdraw from the competition. He is able to rejoin the event with a new team, that has never entered Le Mans before, but torrential rain and technical troubles bring home the danger of endurance racing(n)

programme.title: Jerusalem: The Making of a Holy City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore tells the story of the city from ancient times to the present day and explores its importance in Judaism, Christianity and Islam. He begins in the era of the Canaanites, when Jerusalem first came to be regarded as a sacred site, and examines the evidence that King David made it a Jewish city. He also discusses the construction of the First Temple by Solomon, the life and death of Jesus Christ, and the expulsion of the Jews by the Romans(n)

programme.title: How the Celts Saved Britain
programme.desc: Conclusion. Dan Snow examines the ways in which the Celts saved Britain from cultural oblivion during the Dark Ages. He follows in the footsteps of Ireland's earliest missionaries as they ventured through treacherous barbarian territory, bringing literacy and technology to the future nations of Scotland and England(n)

programme.title: Architects of the Divine: The First Gothic Age
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the development of the Perpendicular style, which took Britain away from the French-inspired buildings of the medieval age. The buildings examined include Gloucester Cathedral, which was built to commemorate a murdered monarch, and King's College Chapel in Cambridge, one of the last buildings to be constructed in the style. Presented by Nina Ramirez(n)

programme.title: How to Be a Surrealist with Philippa Perry
programme.desc: The psychotherapist examines the roots of surrealism, which was established in the political upheavals of 1920s Europe, and looks at new ways of understanding the human psyche(n)

programme.title: Rams: Principles of Good Design
programme.desc: A profile of German Dieter Rams, whose work for consumer products manufacturer Braun and furniture company Vitsoe influenced Apple, but who now regrets being a designer(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: As winter arrives, Iolo endures life-threatening wind chill, ice and snow on the Carneddau high peaks. A cold snap takes him to one of his old roaming grounds on the Berwyn moors, where he finds a solitary robin surviving in thick snow. Opposite Portmeirion, the Dwyryd estuary is teeming with birds feeding. Iolo also heads to Ceunant Llennyrch, a gorge hidden by an ancient woodland that has existed since trees first colonised Snowdonia after the last ice age(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Hurricanes and Heatwaves - The Highs and Lows of British Weather
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the evolution of the weather forecast, from print via radio to TV and beyond, featuring an insight into how the Met Office and the BBC have always used the latest technology to bring the holy grail of accurate forecasting that much closer. Yet, as hand-drawn maps have been replaced by weather apps, the bigger drama of global warming has been playing itself out, as if to prove that people were right all along to obsess about the weather(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history behind the telescope, from the earliest experiments with optics in eighth-century Baghdad to the Hubble Space Telescope. The programme also reveals how Galileo constructed his own telescope and how a New York property boom led to the discovery of ways of measuring a star's distance from Earth(n)

programme.title: How to See a Black Hole: The Universe's Greatest Mystery
programme.desc: For two years BBC cameras have followed, Doctor Sheperd Doeleman of the Smithsonian Astrophysical Observatory and the work of the Event Horizon Telescope project team - a collective of the top scientific minds from around the world. The project combines radio observatories and telescope facilities from around the world to make up a virtual telescope with a diameter spanning the entire planet. This mega-telescope's ultimate mission: to capture the first image ever of a black hole. Although the concept of black holes has been long assumed to be fact, the Event Horizon Telescope's success would definitively prove the existence of this scientific phenomena for the first time - and provide clear visual evidence(n)

programme.title: Jerusalem: The Making of a Holy City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore explores how Jerusalem became a sacred place in Islam, charting Muhammad's relationship with the city, the Crusaders' attempts to reclaim it for Christianity and the construction of holy shrine the Dome of the Rock. He also profiles lesser-known characters in the religion's history, including Al Hakim and Queen Melisende, and discovers parallels between 13th-century peace talks and the current tensions in the Middle East(n)

programme.title: Jane Austen: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley embarks on a road trip across England to take in some of the places Jane Austen lived in and visited during her lifetime. She aims to demonstrate how Jane's personal experiences influenced the central themes of home and property in her novels, and makes use of detective-work to recreate some of the most important locations that no longer stand. She begins in Stoneleigh Abbey, Warwickshire, retracing a journey that marked one of the many times the Austens veered wildly between wealth and genteel poverty - mirroring the Dashwoods of Sense and Sensibility. Lucy also travels from Jane's birthplace in Steventon, Hampshire, to her brother's grand estate at Godmersham Park - the inspiration for Darcy's Pemberley in Pride and Prejudice, along with Lyme Regis, Bath and Southampton(n)

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon charts the history of American art on a coast-to-coast journey, beginning in the east, where he discusses how the country's rise to power was underpinned by the destruction of Native American cultural heritage. He examines portraits of Puritan settlers in Massachusetts, tells the story of a famous painting of Quaker settler William Penn, reads one of the world's most expensive books in Philadelphia and explore the wilderness landscapes that inspired artist Thomas Cole(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Hurricanes and Heatwaves - The Highs and Lows of British Weather
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the evolution of the weather forecast, from print via radio to TV and beyond, featuring an insight into how the Met Office and the BBC have always used the latest technology to bring the holy grail of accurate forecasting that much closer. Yet, as hand-drawn maps have been replaced by weather apps, the bigger drama of global warming has been playing itself out, as if to prove that people were right all along to obsess about the weather(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history behind the telescope, from the earliest experiments with optics in eighth-century Baghdad to the Hubble Space Telescope. The programme also reveals how Galileo constructed his own telescope and how a New York property boom led to the discovery of ways of measuring a star's distance from Earth(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Anni Albers: A Life in Thread
programme.desc: The story of Anni Albers, one of the female Bauhaus pioneers who transformed the perception of textiles into modern art. In 1922, Anni enrolled at the legendary Bauhaus school of art in Weimar Germany to become a painter, but was soon moved into the weaving class which became known as the women's workshop. Here, she joined a group of women who turned the looms to their advantage and used them to create a radical new kind of modern art-textile abstraction. The film also documents Anni's later life in America, where she became one of the world's most famous living artists(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the role played by the Church during Robert the Bruce's 22-year campaign to secure Scottish independence, and how its repeated propaganda campaigns for the cause at home and abroad eventually bore fruit. The programme also examines how the 1320 Declaration of Arbroath ultimately persuaded the Pope to recognise the country as an independent nation(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus 100
programme.desc: A century after it opened its doors, this programme tells the story of the Bauhaus art school in Germany, and the men and women whose designs shaped the modern world. The programme recounts the work of Walter Gropius, architect and founder of the Bauhaus, and the teachers and students he gathered to form the influential school(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus Rules with Vic Reeves
programme.desc: Six graduates from Central St Martins are challenged to create a new work of art, design or performance inspired by artists who taught at the Bauhaus. Key figures from today's worlds of art and design - who have been influenced by the Bauhaus in their own work - set the rules for the experiment. Presented by Vic Reeves(n)

programme.title: Susan Calman's Fringe Benefits
programme.desc: The comedian introduces highlights from the Edinburgh Fringe, going head to head with Scottish mindreader Colin Cloud for some probing chat, as well as getting the best and funniest festival stories from comedians Phoebe Robinson, Nick Helm, Iain Stirling and Jay Lafferty. Game of Thrones actor Daniel Portman and writer Gary McNair also feature(n)

programme.title: Jerusalem: The Making of a Holy City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore charts the history of tension between Muslims, Christian and Jews in the city. He begins by focusing on the Mamluks' period in charge of Jerusalem following the Crusades, before exploring how life changed under the rule of the Islamic Ottoman empire. He also delves into the city's turbulent history during the 19th century, when it became prized by several European powers, and traces the origins of Jerusalem's present-day religious conflicts(n)

programme.title: The Worst Witch
programme.desc: Mildred's having fun jumping high into the air with Enid - using pirated magic to make the grass bouncy. Maud's getting fed up with Enid constantly distracting Mildred - Maud knows Mildred needs to knuckle down if she's going to stand a chance at the academy and can't bear the thought of losing her. Mildred receives a singing teddy from home and it makes her feel homesick. When Mildred looks to Maud for help in Miss Gullet's transformations class, Maud doesn't bail her out and Mildred turns Ethel into a pig instead of a mouse into frog. She doesn't even manage to turn Ethel back successfully - she still has a pig's snout! Mildred receives a harsh warning from Miss Hardbroom who threatens expulsion if she can't perform the spell correctly. Mildred feels fed up with all the rules of school and always being the worst witch. It's the final straw, she wants to go home. Drusilla intercepts her leaving, telling her that Maud and Enid are fighting. But it's a trick, and Mildred finds herself f(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: A Buddhist painter battles the elements at Boudhanath Stupa in Nepal to honour Buddha's birthday, and in Iraq, at Lalish Temple, a young Yazedi woman goes in search of comfort after being traumatised by the recent conflict. At the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem - where a miracle is believed to happen at the same place, at the same time every year - an orthodox Christian delivers a holy flame into the hands of a bishop. Documentary showcasing what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: From Syria to Scotland - Our Lives
programme.desc: The story of Syrian refugee families who have made their homes on the island of Bute in the Firth of Clyde, finding a place of safety after the horrors of the Syrian war they have escaped. Mounzer runs the Orient Salon, named after the busy barber shop he ran in his native Damascus, while next door Rahaf and her husband Fadel run a restaurant and takeaway bringing a taste of Syria to Bute. Morven Christie narrates(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil returns home and is less than impress when Ben throws him a surprise party, preferring to gather information about his attack. He confronts his son about his argument with Jay in the hospital and tells Shirley he has remembered something - prompting a visit to Kat. Meanwhile, Stuart makes it clear he wants to be Callum's best man and Bailey does her best to support Keegan(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian James Acaster, TV presenters Gabby Logan and Melvin Odoom and Cold Feet star Fay Ripley join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Canadian comedian Katherine Ryan is the latest celebrity to dig into her family's past in the popular genealogy documentary. Her investigation leads her to a Methodist minister and his family in colonial Nova Scotia, cod traders in Newfoundland, and eventually to her English roots in Dorset(n)

programme.title: The Day Mountbatten Died
programme.desc: On 27 August, 1979, Lord Louis Mountbatten, great uncle to Prince Charles, was blown up at sea by the IRA off the west coast of Ireland. Three others were killed on the boat that day, including two teenage boys, and later that afternoon the IRA killed 18 British soldiers across the border in Northern Ireland. Forty years on, this is the story of that remarkable Bank Holiday Monday told by those directly affected by it(n)

Evan struggles to adjust to life back in the family home, while the Reardons threaten to detail the Corran Energy deal. DI Breeze continues to put pressure on the Howells family, and Steve begins playing a deadly new game of his own. Wales-set legal drama, starring Eve Myles, Bradley Freegard, Rhashan Stone and Aimee-Ffion Edwards(n)

programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate the fake Botox treatment that left one woman with life changing injuries. Elsewhere, a report on false online reviews that are ruining business across the UK, deadly fake camping stoves that can explode when they are lit, and Michelle tests imitation Airpods headphones(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. In an effort to confront their money-spending ways, Alex and Steph resort to shock tactics, and set up a pop-up-shop in the family home displaying all of their biggest spends, with shocking results. Can Alex and Steph help this family start saving, rather than spending?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: A Buddhist painter battles the elements at Boudhanath Stupa in Nepal to honour Buddha's birthday, and in Iraq, at Lalish Temple, a young Yazedi woman goes in search of comfort after being traumatised by the recent conflict. At the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem - where a miracle is believed to happen at the same place, at the same time every year - an orthodox Christian delivers a holy flame into the hands of a bishop. Documentary showcasing what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: In a first for the programme, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present an appeal for information on behalf of two families whose loved ones have been reported missing to the police. They look at the cases of Matthew Bone, who disappeared in March 2018, and Simon Greaves, who went missing five years ago. They also follow the story of Beryl Everall, who last saw her mother more than 60 years ago when she mysteriously disappeared from her life. All contact with her mother's side of the family was lost, and she was never spoken of again(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emma tells her mum Fiona about Steve being her biological dad, and Ed agrees to let James lend him £5,000, but later Michael finds Ed sat staring at a gambling site. And as Geoff sees his doctor, it is clear there is nothing wrong with him(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Lisa tries to make amends for her part in Lucinda's disappearance but is starting to see a different side to Kate and Guy. Her husband Joe tells her that she should focus on her own family, as their daughter Sally has struggles to cope with the recent events. Guy learns that Sally has been keeping information from the investigation and storms round to Lisa's home. Meanwhile, Detective Constable Joanne Aspinall starts to look into the Rivertys' family history, and grows suspicious of Guy. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Gadsden Correctional Facility near Tallahassee in Florida, where he hears the story of Ashley Humphrey. As a 20-year-old in 2003, she pumped eight bullets into barmaid Sandee Rozzo - a crime for which she is now serving 25 years. Piers tries to understand how she came to commit the crime, wondering whether she was persuaded by her domineering and abusive husband - who was being accused of rape by the victim - or if she is simply a cold-blooded killer who came up with the plan herself(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: In a first for the programme, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present an appeal for information on behalf of two families whose loved ones have been reported missing to the police. They look at the cases of Matthew Bone, who disappeared in March 2018, and Simon Greaves, who went missing five years ago. They also follow the story of Beryl Everall, who last saw her mother more than 60 years ago when she mysteriously disappeared from her life. All contact with her mother's side of the family was lost, and she was never spoken of again(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is assigned to protect an Middle Eastern diplomat who is being pursued by assassins. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. While police are diverted to the affected premises, the crooks target other banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.desc: Maverick San Francisco detective 'Dirty' Harry Callahan sets out to capture a deranged sniper picking off victims at random. Don Siegel's thriller, starring Clint Eastwood, Harry Guardino and Andy Robinson(n)

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Crystal pressures Joyce to sort things out after the pool is contaminated with weedkiller and all guests have to be evacuated, so she hastily arranges a trip to the circus, but Andre the circus manager fails to tell her that the acts are not available. However, Bean, Mateo and Jacqueline come to the rescue and use their connections to help Joyce put on their own evening of show-stopping entertainment(n)

programme.title: Death Race
programme.desc: Jensen Ames is framed for the murder of his wife and sent to Terminal Island Prison, a facility that broadcasts the highly profitable Death Race, a high-octane event where convicts battle to the death in customised cars. When a popular driver dies, crooked prison warden Hennessey persuades Ames to take his place, claiming he will be released if he is victorious. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jason Statham, Joan Allen, Ian McShane and Tyrese Gibson(n)

programme.desc: Animal behaviourist Jo-Rosie Haffenden discusses applying her methods to parenting, and how a greater emphasis on rewarding positive actions than punishing bad behaviour can turn infants into caring and compassionate human beings. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. programme.title: Kathy Burke's All Woman
programme.desc: The actress discusses attitudes to motherhood, discovering why the average age of first-time mothers is rising and contemplating the reasons why women choose whether or not to have children. She talks to a London financial analyst who has decided to freeze her eggs, and discovers the likelihood of the procedure resulting in a successful conception. Kathy also talks to film star Samantha Morton and comedian Katherine Ryan about their experiences of parenthood, and has her first experience of attending a birth(n)

programme.title: Jade: The Reality Star Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Ten years on from her death, this series explores the rollercoaster life of Jade Goody, one of the most loved - and hated - reality TV stars, to tell a bigger story of class, politics and cultural change in Britain, at a time when reality TV became the biggest and most polarising form of entertainment in the country. The second edition focuses on the decisions Jade Goody made which proved disastrous for her life in the spotlight, including a stint on Celebrity Big Brother which left her reputation and business in ruins after a series of abusive rows with Bollywood actress Shilpa Shetty triggered a huge wave of complaints about racist bullying(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay visits family-run Luigi's in Orange County, California, where lazy shortcuts following the retirement of the owner's father have led the business to suffer. Ramsay faces the daunting task of reuniting a feuding family, before turning around the struggling restaurant, which currently has 126 dishes on the menu(n)

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.sub-title: Building Britain's Railways with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain. He begins in the cab of Puffing Billy, the world's oldest-surviving steam locomotive, which was built in 1813 and designed to move coal along a five-mile stretch of track from a Northumberland mine to the docks. Chris also tells the story of Henry Booth, who not only championed George Stephenson's famous Rocket, but also helped finance it, and talks about the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks(n)

programme.title: Big Week at the Zoo
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Nick Baker return to Yorkshire Wildlife Park, near Doncaster, to celebrate the work that British zoos perform in the service of worldwide conservation. In the first edition, they drop in on three one-month-old endangered tiger cubs, Yorkshire Vet star Peter Wright befriends a tame tapir. JB Gill assists as a pygmy hippo is given a much-needed dental procedure and helps determine the sex of a baby sloth, while attempts are made to encourage two rare Chinese alligators to breed(n)

programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the work of Refood, which diverts rotting food from landfill and plugs it into the National Grid. If all the food wasted by UK homes was turned into energy, it could power more than 1.2 million homes. There is also a look at Sue and John Wellfare, who have made it their mission to recycle the unrecyclable. The pair sort items by hand, from biscuit wrappers and baby-food sachets to crisp packets, and have set up drop-off points for what would otherwise end up on the ground(n)

programme.title: Manhunt: Catch Me if You Can
programme.desc: The tactical police officers go in search of a convicted murderer who is suspected of committing a serious assault while out on licence from prison, hunting day and night through squats, hostels and homeless haunts. They also mount a surveillance operation on a man thought to be a member of a gang who committed an attempted burglary and go on the trail of a serial shoplifter who failed to turn up at court(n)

programme.title: Inside the World's Toughest Prisons
programme.sub-title: Secrets of the World's Toughest Prison
programme.desc: Journalist Paul Connolly experiences life as an inmate in some of the world's most notorious prisons. In this instalment, he serves time with some of Poland's most dangerous criminals in Piotrkow, a maximum security facility where isolation, strict discipline and zero tolerance keep prisoners locked up for 23 hours a day. Paul learns first-hand about the punishment room where violent inmates are subdued, is subject to an intimate body search, watches as the guards search for contraband, and discovers why inmates are forced to eat meals in their cells(n)

programme.title: Extreme Flights: Out of Control
programme.desc: Exploring what happens when flights lose control, including a packed passenger jet that burst into flames just moments from take-off and the family flight that ended in disaster when a mid-air collision left passengers free-falling through burning debris at 12,000 feet(n)

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.sub-title: Building Britain's Railways with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines the role of the railways during the two global conflicts of the 20th century, keeping vital supply lines open and transporting troops to and from the front. In the First World War, railway works were converted into munitions factories and women were employed for these dangerous jobs and their heroic efforts helped win them the vote, while in the Second World War, they were vital in organising the evacuation of a million children and offered underground shelters from air raids(n)

programme.title: Big Week at the Zoo
programme.desc: The Yorkshire Vet's Julian Norton assists with a procedure on a giant Aldabra tortoise, while Helen Skelton helps to look after the only family of polar bears in captivity in the UK. One of the zoo's male Asiatic lions is being transported to Tehran, but is almost immediately replaced by a newly arrived female(n)

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: Raven chicks are born in the tower for the first time in 30 years - but only one of the newborn birds can stay, and ravenmaster Chris faces the tough decision of choosing one to keep and finding suitable homes for all the others. The tower director prepares to retire after 15 years, and takes a moment to enjoy his final gun salute, while AJ Clarke, only the second woman to join the Yeoman warders, embarks on a fitness regime that soon leaves her in need of a new uniform. Plus, a look at the story of the time the future Queen Elizabeth I was imprisoned in the tower for plotting to overthrow her sister, Queen Mary(n)

programme.title: Mick Philpott: Britain's Most Evil Father
programme.desc: In May 2012, Mick Philpott started a fire in his own house which killed six children, five of them his own, and was jailed for life for manslaughter in April the following year. This documentary looks at the aftermath of the tragedy and features interviews with one of Philpott's sons, close family members, friends and neighbours(n)

Under scrutiny this week are BBC drama series Doctor Foster, classical music feast the Last Night of the Proms, Shane Meadows' mini-series This Is England '90, singing showcase The X Factor, Britain's Biggest Adventures with Bear Grylls and hook-up show First Dates. Narrated by Caroline Aherne. First aired in September 2015(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Room inspections uncover all manner of unsightly problems, but a tour of nearby Knowsley Safari Park gives the contestants a chance to enjoy themselves(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Julian Banner-Price host the competition at their expensive B&B Plas Dinas Country House in Snowdonia, giving their new guests the priciest rooms. Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Annette McAnespie welcomes the group to Hazelwood Farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, for the final B&B visit. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time at Annette McAnespie's farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the week's winner is announced(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer guide more prospective property buyers through the complex world of viewings and agents. David and Claire Wainwright have £460,000 to find a family home in Bournemouth, Dorset, to help them recreate the relaxed, beachside lifestyle they enjoyed in Australia. New parents Matt and Jodie Annen are also looking for a family home, but can't agree on where it should be. With £350,000 to spend, she thinks Bournemouth would offer them all they need, while he has his heart set on the pricier postcodes of neighbouring Poole(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Florence and Nadine, two of Chester's Grevy's zebras, are about to give birth, but other animals are struggling to produce offspring, including Iblis the Asiatic lion. Meanwhile, warthog dad Magnum is feeling the pressure of parenting as two of his older children, Dobby and Neville, are becoming increasingly hard to control, and Magnum is losing his hair with the stress(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. Elsewhere, a Springer Spaniel all the way from Guernsey is brought in for spinal surgery, and a Caucasian Shepherd puppy called Bear requires two hip replacements(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel redesigns the rooms in one of the chateau's turrets with a fruits and flowers motif - but an unexpected surprise lurks behind her creations. Outside, Dick is putting together a parkour fitness trail in the 12 acres of grounds, but hits upon an unexpected obstacle buried deep underground(n)

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Control Enthusiast
programme.desc: A stand-up performance by the comedian, including topics such as road rage, IBS, her favourite word and how she lets her husband know 'tonight's the night'. There are also stories about 'Rescue Men', passing wind in hospital pants, what can happen at a bra fitting and how she feels about her belly(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old Josh is flown in with a possible penile fracture resulting from a canal barge accident, and while his parents make jokes to try to lighten the atmosphere, the injury could have serious repercussions for his future. A man with an injured finger following a night of clubbing contemplates his upcoming gender reassignment therapy, which will include taking hormones, while an 80-year-old with terminal cancer arrives after collapsing at her home(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the rise in violent crime across the UK and the increase in attacks on police officers. PCs Matt Ambrose and Mike Lowe are dispatched to a house in Crewe following reports of a man threatening his ex-partner with a gun, and later are called to a local sports club where a brawl is underway and they find themselves dangerously outnumbered. PC Claire Heatley arrests a male motorcyclist suspected of drink-driving, but becomes the victim of a vicious assault when the suspect headbutts her(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: Wildfires in Paradise, California, the deadliest in the state's history, wipe out homes, towns and lives. Calls tell the stories of the emergency responders and those who escaped(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo

Meanwhile, warthog dad Magnum is feeling the pressure of parenting as two of his older children, Dobby and Neville, are becoming increasingly hard to control, and Magnum is losing his hair with the stress(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Thailand to find out if the alarming headlines about additive MSG are justified, Kate Quilton heads deep into the Amazon rainforest to investigate rumours that the Brazil nuts on UK supermarket shelves are radioactive and Matt Tebutt reports on a Lincolnshire business producing 1.5 million bottles of elderflower cordial without employing a single flower picker(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and the team spring to the aid of a couple from Wembley, north London, who own a flat but almost no furniture(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates the catastrophic effects of not getting enough vitamin D, meeting a young boy suffering from rickets and discovering the surprising origin of the fortified vitamin D that's added to many supermarket foods. Kate Quilton explores how supermarkets are able to sell normally expensive wagyu beef at knockdown prices, while Matt Tebbutt heads to Switzerland to ask why there are holes in Swiss cheese(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Jonathan and Michael prepare for a wedding day with 45 guests, and a swimming pool continues to cause problems for Mandy and Steve(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen host the next stage of the patisserie contest, in which the three best teams from both sets of heats are brought together, facing new rivals and yet more demanding challenges as they strive to impress judges Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin. For their first task, the six teams must each prepare 24 classic croissants and 24 Viennoiserie in just four hours. The second challenge involves preparing a romantic sugar showpiece, which must incorporate two types of confectionery(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and the Wirral, where items include a 1920s dance hall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The sports car-themed PitStop in Hertfordshire hosts the first round, as owners Melvyn and Sindy Rutter try to make a good impression. However, the absence of tea and coffee in the rooms does not go down well with the guests, and Melvyn's after-dinner talk about his collection of Morgan cars fails to spark much interest(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gill and Peter Tydie invite their fellow competitors to stay at the Victory in Mersea, Essex, a four-star B&B with 26 members of staff to run the bar, restaurant and accommodation. A trip to a local fishery gives one contestant an opportunity to annoy his peers, and the discovery of a hole in a bedsheet leaves the hosts feeling uneasy about their chances of success(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave Botha hosts at his Butterfly Paragliding B&B on the Isle of Wight. Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham are unhappy they must sleep in a tepee in the garden and use an outdoor toilet, and claim they were served watery eggs and rubbery sausages for breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham host the final night at Braddon Hall in Torquay, Devon, where Melvyn and Sindy take issue with their squeaky four-poster bed. The hoteliers try to entertain their guests by inviting them to take part in an archery contest - which ends in accusations of unfair play(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. One of the hosts gets upset about large underpayments and a couple interrogate their guests about the criticism in their feedback, before the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer helps John and Stella move from Hampshire to Scotland, searching for a home with a view in the rural Lowlands close to Loch Lomond. Kirstie Allsopp is with Mikey and Anna, who are looking for a flat in the West End of Glasgow. However, so far they have fallen foul of the competitive market in a search that has become something of a family affair(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker offer advice to Georgia and John and their three children, who are feeling cramped in their current home in the Brisbane suburb of Corinda(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the behaviour of the animals at Chester Zoo. Kendari the babirusa - a bizarre-looking creature also known as a deer-pig - is trying to give birth, but is being disturbed by a couple of mischievous otters. Young male orang-utan Willie struggles to impress the females, while Raz the aye-aye, a rare nocturnal primate related to lemurs, is being encouraged to share his home with two jumping rats(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: In the first of a new series, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat a giant Newfoundland puppy with a painful knee and shoulder. What appears straightforward quickly turns into a much bigger concern, and Noel and his team are challenged to invent not one but two new implants for her. Meanwhile, a rescue black Labrador called Moogi is in need of treatment for severe elbow arthritis, and a golden Labrador born with a deformed front paw is offered a bionic solution in the form of a unique, custom-made prosthesis(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The temperatures soar as Dick and Angel face the busiest period of the year, as the school holidays coincide with peak wedding season. Dick comes up with an ingenious solution to stop wedding cakes from melting in the heat, but it involves piercing the chateau's walls. The moat is transformed into a boating lake, giving visitors a chance to cool down, and the family takes to the water in a home-made punt(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: In Kent, Mark Evans meets Lee and Justine Oakley, who bought a rusting 90ft cargo hull and plan to turn it into a four-bedroom home on the River Medway, near Rochester(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ninety-three-year-old Ernest is brought in with life-threatening injuries after being knocked down by a hit-and-run driver just outside the hospital. He goes into cardiac arrest, and a consultant must keep his heart going manually in a bid to save his life. Meanwhile, an 83-year-old's finger is mangled by a machine at his metal-working business, and a 51-year-old man with learning difficulties is treated after collapsing, prompting his elderly mother to wonder how he will eventually cope after her death(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Spain to meet an entrepreneur who is investing a large amount of money in an attempt to farm the critically endangered Atlantic bluefin tuna. With just one of these fish selling for a record £1million at auction in Tokyo, the rewards could be huge. Kate Quilton investigates why ice cream in freezers can sometimes get gritty ahead of the best before date, while Matt Tebbutt tries to find out what the difference is between the supermarkets' premium and budget range of frozen peas(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge helps Donna McDougal, who believes a horde of ancient armour was hidden in her chateau's lake. programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole is rather taken with a throne salvaged from an exotic nightclub and Simon O'Brien's restoration of a Victorian doll's house fails to go to plan(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

The hostess's pet cats are not to all of her guests' liking, and with activities including a tour of a feline cemetery, the theme for the stay is made abundantly clear(n)

programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are the third hosts, at their three-bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, Cornwall. Some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room but the food is a success, and Eileen Finn demonstrates her karate skills on the men(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Cavendish in Bournemouth, run by Tim and Karen Sturgess. However, Eileen is delighted by the feline memorabilia in her room and the group enjoys a spot of indoor paint-balling and an open-top bus tour of the resort(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. programme.title: Location, Location, Location

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

programme.title: Outlander

programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers.
